title: erysimum capitatum var. lompocense
intro: Erysimum capitatum var. lompocense, also known as Lompoc wallflower, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant that is native to the Lompoc area in California. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Erysimum capitatum var. lompocense include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and are often used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems.
